summary: Install-time code execution via bin-shadowing of `node` during Bun installs
details: Package declares `"bin": {"node": "./shim.js"}` in package.json, which causes the package manager to place a `node`-named entry in `node_modules/.bin/` that shadows the system `node` binary during dependency installation. The package also declares a dependency on esbuild (a default-trusted package under Bun whose postinstall script runs `node install.js`), so esbuild's postinstall invokes the attacker-controlled shim instead of the real `node` interpreter. The result is arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at install time with no victim-side lifecycle scripts required — a plain `bun i evil-pkg` in an empty project is sufficient to trigger shim.js. The shipped shim.js writes a marker file to /tmp during install; the same execution primitive can read installer secrets (env vars, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc) or fetch a further payload. Self-labeling as a proof-of-concept does not change the fact that the package is published and weaponized on the public registry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
